title: Objects, Prototypes, Properties, and Data Shape · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Model service records and shared behavior while defending against accidental mutation and unsafe property input. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a validated service-record domain model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, read, update, delete, enumerate, and compute properties while distinguishing own and inherited state.
Use object destructuring, rest, and spread while accounting for shallow copies, accessors, and overwrite order.
Trace property lookup and method sharing through the prototype chain without class-only mental models.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Object Properties evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Object Properties assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Build job "J-4" from validated input, compose retry and notification policies, and prove an inherited or delegated method reads the intended receiver without sharing mutable defaults.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Object Properties has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Object Properties precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Build job "J-4" from validated input, compose retry and notification policies, and prove an inherited or delegated method reads the intended receiver without sharing mutable defaults.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: In and Object.hasOwn report exactly the same property set.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested
Trace values, bindings, receivers, mutations, and queued work before editing code
Implement the smallest behavior and retain an assertion over the actual result
Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Prototype Pollution: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Build job "J-2" from validated input, compose retry and notification policies, and prove an inherited or delegated method reads the intended receiver without sharing mutable defaults.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate external keys and use safe containers or allowlists to prevent prototype-pollution paths.
